---
title: "The ExPdPy apps"
---

::: {.callout-tip appearance="simple"}
**No installation required** — try an app live in your browser:

<a href="https://expdpy.streamlit.app/" class="btn btn-primary" target="_blank" rel="noopener" role="button">☁️ Launch ExPdPy on Streamlit Cloud →</a>
:::

expdpy ships **three** no-code [Streamlit](https://streamlit.io/) apps — one per module —
that share the same shell (data upload, the analysis-sample pipeline, config save/load and
reproducible notebook export) and differ only in which pages they expose:

- **Explore** — exploratory data analysis (tables, distributions, trends, scatter…),
- **Analyze** — panel estimators (regression, FWL, panel models, CRE, event study…),
- **Learn** — the teaching layer (concept sandboxes and concept explainers).

They are provided by the `streamlit` extra:

```bash
pip install "expdpy[streamlit]"
# or, with uv:
uv pip install "expdpy[streamlit]"
```

## Launching from Python

Each app has its own launcher. Pass a DataFrame (or nothing, for the dataset picker):

```python
from expdpy.streamlit_app import ExploreApp, AnalyzeApp, LearnApp
from expdpy.data import load_kuznets, load_kuznets_data_def, get_config

ExploreApp(
    load_kuznets(),
    df_def=load_kuznets_data_def(),     # identifies the panel dimensions (country x year)
    config_list=get_config("kuznets"),  # opens on the N-shaped Kuznets curve
)

AnalyzeApp(load_kuznets(), df_def=load_kuznets_data_def())
LearnApp()  # the sandboxes and explainers need no data
```

Each launcher serializes your data to a temporary bundle and starts `streamlit run` in a
subprocess, opening a browser. The **sidebar** holds the global pipeline — dataset/sample
picker, file upload, subset filter, outlier treatment, user-defined variables, and config
save/load + notebook export — and the module's analysis components are grouped into pages you
switch between in the navigation menu.

![The Explore app's Overview & Data page on the kuznets panel.](images/streamlit-overview.png)

## The pages

Each app exposes the pages for its module, ordered as the [Explore](explore.qmd) case study
runs: *know the panel → describe it → split its variation → trends → groups → relationships →
dynamics*. Time-series and panel-only pages and components (Trends, Within & between, Dynamics,
missing values, by-group trend, event study, panel models) are **hidden automatically for
cross-sectional data**.

### Explore app

| Page | Components |
|---|---|
| **Overview & Data** | sample preview, panel balance & coverage, missing-value heatmap, value heatmap |
| **Describe variables** | descriptive statistics, histogram, category bar chart, extreme observations |
| **Within & between** | within/between (`xtsum`) variation, per-unit trajectories (spaghetti) |
| **Trends** | variable trend (1–3 series), quantile trend, distribution over time |
| **By group** | group means (bar), distribution (violin), group means over time |
| **Relationships** | correlation table + heatmap, scatter with color/size/LOESS, within-vs-between scatter |
| **Dynamics** | state transition matrix, within-unit persistence |

### Analyze app

| Page | Components |
|---|---|
| **Regression** | OLS with fixed effects and clustered standard errors — with plain-language interpretation, a method explainer, a coefficient plot and the Frisch–Waugh–Lovell plot |
| **Event study & DiD** | staggered difference-in-differences / event study and the treatment-structure panel view (panel data) |
| **Panel models** | pooled / between / fixed / random effects, the Hausman test, and correlated random effects (Mundlak) (panel data) |

### Learn app

| Page | Components |
|---|---|
| **Concept sandboxes** | simulated teaching demos — omitted-variable bias, pooled-vs-fixed-effects, clustering, first differences, within vs LSDV |
| **Concept explainers** | a browsable index of every concept explainer (`list_topics()` / `explain()`) |

![The Analyze app's Regression page: the cubic in log GDP per capita recovers the N — a native coefficient table with significance stars and fit statistics.](images/streamlit-regression.png)

## The dataset picker and upload

Launched without data — or deployed to the Cloud — an app starts with a **dataset picker**
(defaulting to Kuznets; also Gapminder) and a file uploader. An uploaded CSV / Excel / Parquet
file overrides the selected dataset:

```python
from expdpy.streamlit_app import ExploreApp

ExploreApp()  # bundled-dataset picker + upload box
```

### Cross-sectional data

Omit the time-series identifier and the time-trend pages/components disappear:

```python
ExploreApp(load_kuznets().query("year == 2025"))  # no time id -> cross-sectional
```

### The sample pipeline

The sidebar's **Subset by** / **Value** selectors restrict the sample to one level of a factor,
and **Outlier treatment** winsorizes or truncates the numeric columns. Every component
downstream reflects the prepared sample.

### User-defined variables

Expand **Advanced: user-defined variables** to compute new variables from the base data with
safe expressions (column references plus `+ - * / ** %`, comparisons, `& |`, and the functions
`isna`, `exp`, `log`, `lag`, `lead`; `lag`/`lead` are panel-aware).

::: {.callout-note}
Expressions are evaluated with a restricted AST walker — never `eval`/`exec`.
:::

### Saving configurations and reproducible export

**Save config** downloads the current selections as JSON; **Load config** restores them.
**Export notebook + data** downloads a zip with the prepared sample (parquet) plus a Jupyter
notebook and a `.py` script that recreate every displayed component with `expdpy` calls.

## Console commands

The `streamlit` extra installs three console scripts, one per app:

```bash
expdpy-explore    # the Explore app
expdpy-analyze    # the Analyze app
expdpy-learn      # the Learn app
```

## Deploying on Streamlit Community Cloud

The repository ships a dedicated entry script for each app — point the Cloud at one of them
(or run it locally):

```bash
streamlit run app_explore.py
streamlit run app_analyze.py
streamlit run app_learn.py
```

A combined `streamlit_app.py` is also provided: it shows a small landing page that lets the
user pick a module, and can be pinned to one app by setting the `EXPDPY_MODULE` environment
variable (`explore` / `analyze` / `learn`).

```bash
streamlit run streamlit_app.py
```

::: {.callout-tip}
On Streamlit Community Cloud, set the app's main file to one of the `app_*.py` scripts and add
`expdpy` (with the `streamlit` extra) to the environment. The app starts with the dataset
picker and upload box — no code required.
:::

::: {.callout-note}
Upload size is capped by `.streamlit/config.toml` (`[server] maxUploadSize`, default 200 MB).
Increase it there, or pass `max_upload_size=` to a launcher for a local run.
:::

## Customizing the apps

The three launchers (`ExploreApp`, `AnalyzeApp`, `LearnApp`) accept the same customization
arguments.

### Selecting components

`components` controls which analysis components appear (and seeds page selection). Pass a list
or a `{name: bool}` mapping:

```python
from expdpy.streamlit_app import ExploreApp
from expdpy.data import load_kuznets, load_kuznets_data_def

ExploreApp(
    load_kuznets(),
    df_def=load_kuznets_data_def(),
    components=["descriptive_table", "scatter_plot", "corrplot"],
)
```

The available component names are:

`bar_chart`, `missing_values`, `descriptive_table`, `histogram`, `ext_obs`,
`by_group_bar_graph`, `by_group_violin_graph`, `trend_graph`, `quantile_trend_graph`,
`by_group_trend_graph`, `corrplot`, `scatter_plot`, `regression`, `event_study`,
`panel_models`.

A navigation page is shown only when at least one of its components is active and it belongs to
that app's module, and time-series components are dropped for cross-sectional data.

### Advanced mode — building analysis variables

Provide a `var_def` frame (or fill the sidebar editor) to compute the analysis sample from the
base data. Each `var_def` is a safe expression (`isna`, `exp`, `log`, `lag`, `lead`,
arithmetic, comparisons, `& |`); `lag`/`lead` shift within `entity` groups ordered by `time`.

```python
import pandas as pd
from expdpy.streamlit_app import AnalyzeApp
from expdpy.data import load_kuznets, load_kuznets_data_def

var_def = pd.DataFrame(
    {
        "var_name": ["country", "year", "gdp_pc_growth"],
        "var_def": ["country", "year", "(gdp_pc - lag(gdp_pc, 1)) / lag(gdp_pc, 1)"],
        "type": ["entity", "time", "numeric"],
        "can_be_na": [False, False, True],
    }
)
AnalyzeApp(load_kuznets(), df_def=load_kuznets_data_def(), var_def=var_def)
```

### Disabling features and server options

```python
ExploreApp(
    load_kuznets(),
    df_def=load_kuznets_data_def(),
    export_nb_option=False,      # hide notebook export
    save_settings_option=False,  # hide config save/load
    headless=True,               # forwarded to `streamlit run --server.headless true`
    port=8765,                   # `--server.port 8765`
)
```

Server-style keyword arguments (`port`, `host`, `headless`, `max_upload_size`, …) are mapped to
the corresponding `streamlit run --server.*` flags. Pass `run=False` to get the command list
back without launching (useful in tests).
